task_completion.description: Verify the agent runs the OpenICE simulation with the required simulated medical devices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenICE GUI window open showing the 'Simulated Multiparameter Monitor' and 'Simulated Pulse Oximeter' running, or terminal commands executing the OpenICE application.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accesses or processes the specific OpenICE log file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands (e.g., grep, awk, cat) or a script (e.g., Python) explicitly reading from '/home/ga/openice/logs/openice.log'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the target CSV file on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'vital_signs_dataset.csv' visible on the Desktop GUI, or terminal output showing the file exists at '/home/ga/Desktop/vital_signs_dataset.csv'.

task_completion.description: Verify the CSV file contains the exact required headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the CSV file opened in a text editor, spreadsheet viewer, or printed in the terminal (e.g., via 'head' or 'cat') showing the first line is exactly 'Timestamp,Metric,Value'.

task_completion.description: Verify the CSV file contains extracted Heart Rate and SpO2 data values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the contents of the CSV file showing multiple rows of data containing timestamps, metric identifiers (either 'HeartRate'/'SpO2' or the raw codes 'MDC_ECG_HEART_RATE'/'MDC_PULS_OXIM_SAT_O2'), and numeric values.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent extracts data programmatically rather than manually typing fake data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of command-line text processing tools (grep, awk, sed) or a custom script (Python, bash) to parse the log file. The agent should not be seen manually typing rows of data into a text editor.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent generates real synthetic data by running the simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence that the OpenICE application was actually launched and interacted with to generate the log file, rather than the agent just creating a mock log file from scratch.

integrity.description: Ensure the final CSV is saved to the exact requested path.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the file path in the text editor, terminal commands, or file manager to confirm it is exactly '/home/ga/Desktop/vital_signs_dataset.csv' and not a temporary directory or different filename.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires extracting data from OpenICE, an open-source medical device interoperability platform. The agent must run simulated devices (Multiparameter Monitor and Pulse Oximeter) to generate logs at '/home/ga/openice/logs/openice.log'. The target metrics use IEEE 11073 standard codes: MDC_ECG_HEART_RATE (Heart Rate) and MDC_PULS_OXIM_SAT_O2 (SpO2). The final output must be a CSV at '/home/ga/Desktop/vital_signs_dataset.csv' with exactly the header 'Timestamp,Metric,Value' and contain at least 30 rows of extracted data.
